Introducing RISESCREENER by @risechain  @risextrade 

https://t.co/3D9wfoahVE

V1.5 is now live. V1.0 was a simple demo focused on "functionality" — once I confirmed that every feature worked, I moved quickly into a design upgrade.

I've incorporated the community's requested changes and are unveiling a fully upgraded RISE Screener.

It's organized into 7 tabs:

1. Overview — Get a complete exchange snapshot, including TVL/Volume/OI along with Top OI / Volume / Gainers / Losers.
2. Markets — Price and 24h change for every market listed on RISEx, plus funding rates and max leverage.
3. Fees/Liquidations — Track liquidations on RISEx and the fees generated from trading.
4. Traders — A window into RISEx's top traders. Because RISEx is fully on-chain, you can see the 24h volume / unrealized PnL / OI of the top n traders. Dive into the details — their maker/taker activity, estimated liquidation prices, and more.
5. Flows — Spot new flow through deposit/withdrawal data moving in and out of RISEx.
6. Network — For anyone curious about RISE Chain: live-data-driven animations and explanations. Learn the theory behind why RISE Chain is so fast.
7. Explorer — Look up RISE Chain blocks and individual txs, and feel the speed for yourself. (You'll probably struggle just to follow it with your eyes.)

You can switch between light/dark mode using the toggle at the top.

Copy trading on @risextrade  

Just follow the top-performing wallets.

Continuously monitor the top 3 wallets by top PnL, OI, and volume on RISEx. Or, if you prefer, you can search for a specific wallet and copy it.

In this video, we search for a specific wallet and demonstrate copy trading actually working — with near-zero copy latency.

Someone asked me why I'm bullish on @risextrade , and here are a few reasons I can quickly list:
- CLOB is fully on-chain, which is what I value most. It's the highest industry standard right now.

- It integrates directly into @risechain , an L2 token of ETH. What does this mean? You own a fully on-chain CLOB integrated directly into an ETH L2 token, which opens the door to access to capital and integration capabilities. Many people will argue, "Oh, isn't L2 dead?" Actually, L2 isn't dead; it's just being misused.

You have a high-speed L2 EVM that can integrate a fully on-chain CLOB token; just thinking about it allows you to build so many cool things on it.

Okay, so how is it different from Lighter?

Lighter is an Ethereum L2 token with custom ZK circuits for verifiable matching and liquidations. Essentially, they don't have an EVM yet and are still in the development process. Whether their EVM, once completed, will be able to communicate atomically with their CLOB is still unconfirmed, which is crucial for opening up the possibility of integration.

What does Risex need to succeed?

- Liquidity is a prerequisite for successful peer-to-peer marketing, especially for a CLOB; launching liquidity for the markets is even more necessary and expensive.

Therefore, attracting users and market makers is paramount. No matter how good a product is in terms of design and architecture, without liquidity and users, it will ultimately fail.

That's enough. My final piece of advice: Don't just read and listen, try it out. Research is good, but don't just stop at reading and listening. Using and experiencing a new product yourself will give you the most accurate and objective perspective.

I hope to see more applications that can leverage CLOB fully on-chain in a completely atomic way on @risextrade . That would be a breakthrough in a deFi landscape that is increasingly exhausted in terms of both ideas and productivity.

Durante las próximas horas tengo una reunión con ellos y espero sacar información relevante para nosotros, especialmente sobre si existe una season oculta, cuánto puede durar la campaña de puntos, qué tipo de actividad ponderará más y cómo podemos optimizar nuestra estrategia desde el principio.

➡️La idea es sencilla: generar volumen ahora que estamos early, conseguir la mayor cantidad posible de puntos antes de que el mercado despierte y, cuando llegue el fomo, estar tranquilos viendo cómo el retail se pelea por conseguir muchos menos puntos que nosotros. 

➡️¿Qué tiene RiseX que no tengan otros perps nuevos? La respuesta corta es que RiseX aspira a mantener el orderbook completamente onchain e integrado con DeFi, pero sin sacrificar rendimiento. Si lo consiguen, estaríamos hablando de una infraestructura que combine trading  con composabilidad real con DeFi.

➡️Obviamente, ya sabéis cómo actuar. Delta neutral si no queréis exponeros direccionalmente, o trading orgánico si tenéis una tesis clara de mercado y sabéis lo que estáis haciendo. Lo importante es generar actividad con cabeza, no volverse loco, no sobreoperar y no quemar liquidez por perseguir puntos sin calcular el coste real.

➡️Yo voy a destinar una pequeña parte de mi portfolio para generar volumen en RiseX. En esta fase del mercado, lo más importante sigue siendo sobrevivir, mantenerse líquido y no destruir tu cuenta operando a lo loco, pero dicho esto, creo que estamos ante un muy buen momento para empezar a darle caña mientras la oportunidad sigue siendo poco competida.

➡️Mi objetivo inicial será hacer alrededor de 1M de volumen semanal. Esta semana ya he cumplido con 1M y encima he cerrado unos $300 de profit shorteando BTC, así que de momento no podía empezar mejor.

Se rumorea Season Oculta de Puntos de RiseX

Ya sabemos qué pasó en la season oculta de Aster hace justo un año….

New value prop may be flying under the radar.

In case you were unaware, @HyperliquidX and @risextrade are converging to the same endpoint of CEX-grade perps natively composable with general EVM DeFi, but from opposite starting points. 

Hyperliquid is currently building backwards in that it’s retrofitting general composability onto an exchange native L1 (HyperCore and HyperEVM are bifurcated, with bridging/precompile seams between them). 
@risextrade is novel in that it is putting the book in the execution layer. Your assets sit in one place and are fully defi composable to any app built on the chain. Same state, no separation. So your assets can sit and fund perps margin while also earning yield while also being used for options.

Not saying it will compare to hyperliquid just noting they’re trying to do the same thing architecturally, with Rise having the full package natively. Thats the goal anyway. Early days for Rise, could be a worthwhile farm. Have a few ref’s left to give if interested.

13 Things Everyone Should Know Before Supporting @risextrade 

1) They celebrate $1B cumulative volume... while a regular perp aggregator reached the same milestone in a single week, without an MM.

2) They compare themselves to Hyperliquid... while Hyperliquid compares itself to Binance.

3) They sold a private Kaito community round by teasing a Q4 2025 TGE... it's now the end of Q2 2026.

4) They hired a CIS Community Lead... who never even joined Discord to say hello.

5) They post pictures of food... while their community is starving for updates.

6) They ask the community for feedback... then leave a 👍 and change nothing.

7) They host YouTube AMAs... while ignoring live questions in favor of pre-selected ones.

8) Their BD blocks some of the most valuable community members... while paying influencers who call the project Gigagas L2 instead of RiseX.

9) They keep telling everyone how great the platform is... while there's virtually no liquidity.

10) They build hype around announcements... only for the announcement to be: "Mainnet is currently in maintenance mode."

11) They advertise $10,000 competitions where the top prize is supposed to be $1,500... yet people end up receiving $50.

12) They claim to have a huge active community... while most of the activity comes from moderators.

13) They expect the community to buy NFTs, invest in private rounds, spend hours keeping a dead Discord alive, and publish two posts every week.

Meanwhile the team gets salaries.

The community pays with money.

The community pays with time.

And transparency is treated like an optional feature.

 : 0.144x -> it means early, high turnover

what do all these mean for perp DEXs out there?

CFTC’s historic regulatory breakthrough has completely rewritten the landscape for perpetual decentralized exchanges Perp DEXs On-chain platforms like @risextrade, @HyperliquidX, now face an entirely new set of market dynamics, competitive threats, and structural opportunities

now you might be wondering what these means for perp dexs, are they gonna start losing momentum?

the CFTC ruling does not kill DeFi derivatives, instead, it formalizes a permanent, two-sided market split,

while legal onshore options like Coinbase and Kalshi will capture risk-averse institutional money, they cannot replicate the core structural mechanics that make Perp DEXs attractive to native crypto traders

here's the thing,Onchain platforms possess foundational structural advantages that centralized, regulated entities cannot legally or technologically offer

1: Instant Listing of "Long Tail" Assets: Regulated US platforms must clear every single asset through a grueling case by case review process

 If a brand new token launches, Coinbase may take months or years to approve a perp for it, In contrast, Hyperliquid’s HIP-3 framework allows permissionless listing of any asset with a price feed instantly

2: No Counterparty or Credit Risk: the FTX collapse is a good example

but on platforms like RISEx, the liquidations, order matching, and safety vaults are completely transparent and automated on the blockchain, traders can audit the exchange’s solvency in real time, rather than blindly trusting a corporation's internal books

3: Global Pre-IPO and Synthetic Asset Access: DeFi platforms are morphing into comprehensive, 24/7 global synthetic asset engines, for example, when SpaceX listed on Nasdaq in early June 2026, Hyperliquid's 'SPCX' stock-linked perpetual contract instantly exploded to $1.4 billion in a single day's trading volume
 A US regulated platform would face massive SEC roadblocks trying to offer high-leverage synthetic equities to retail traders

The @risechain @risextrade - Most people describe Shreds as a faster confirmation mechanism. 

I think that's underselling what's actually happening.

Shreds transform blockchain execution from a batch system into a streaming system.  

1. Traditional blockchains operate like batch processors.

🔹Transactions wait.
🔹A block forms.
🔹The block finishes.
🔹Then the network responds. 

State updates arrive in bursts.  

2. Shreds change the timing model. 

Instead of waiting for a completed block, execution state is propagated continuously as work is performed.

Confirmation becomes a stream not an event.  

3. This matters because latency is not just a UX problem. It's a coordination problem.

Market makers and liquidators and arbitrage systems and traders all depend on how quickly shared state updates propagate through the network.  

4. The interesting part is not simply that confirmations arrive in ~1ms. 

It's that economically secured state information becomes available before block completion.

That reduces the gap between execution and awareness.  

5. Viewed this way Shreds are not merely making blocks faster. 

They are reducing the importance of the block as the primary unit of interaction.

The network starts behaving less like a sequence of batches and more like a continuous real time system.

That's a deeper architectural shift than speed alone.

The @risechain @risextrade - Most perp DEXs run on black box execution. 

You place an order and You pray it fills where you expect and You hope slippage does not eat you alive and You trust a system that shows you nothing.

RISEx breaks that pattern entirely.

Fully onchain order book every fill is verifiable and every depth is real and every execution is transparent by default. 

🔹No AMM approximations hiding behind a clean UI.
🔹No off chain matching you can not audit.
🔹Just honest market structure on RISE. 

And for risk management? 

TP/SL is native not a third party workaround and not a bot you are running on the side and not a plugin hoping it catches your level in time. 

It's built in and It executes onchain. It works the way it should.

🔹Speed without opacity.
🔹Control without complexity.
🔹Execution without the prayer. 

This is what perp trading infrastructure should have looked like from day one.

This became easily my #1 tool for get the full level picture of  everything happening on @risextrade.

It is called Rise Screener and  is basically Dune Analytics on steroids, specifically focusing on RiseX on-chain data.

Everything in one clean dashboard:
▪️Volume stats (24h + all-time)
▪️Open Interest
▪️Funding rates + max lev
▪️Fees & Liquidations
▪️TVL & flows
▪️Top traders (volume, OI, PnL)
▪️Markets overview, gainers/losers, upcoming/existing markets

you name it, and it's there. 

I also liked the latest summary feature, providing quick recap of main KPIs, refreshed daily (screen below).

Big kudos @0x_snatch for  building this absolute graal for analyzing on-chain data. 
This is a great addition to existing native tools on @risechain.

Gonna dive into exploring it more, you can check it yourself here https://t.co/amIfaqwYZn.

cheers, and never stop rising

vTokens
You lend capital to professional market makers that trade on Perp DEXes and generate points + yield

ST/EPT
1 vToken = 1 ST + 1 EPT

ST => Strategy Token. This contains the principal + yield side of the vToken
EPT => Expected Points Token. This contains the points (or credits as we refer to it now) side of the vToken

If you want higher yield or points exposure than what's available on vTokens, you can directly buy STs or EPTs from sellers available on the market.

@risextrade a fully onchain perp exchange built on @risechain. It came out of testnet barely a month ago, which means we're early - really early

Why, out of all the dexes out there, i singled @risextrade?  

• Points aren't live yet. Launch expected late may. So right now is the last window to farm retroactive rewards before the program starts the official start

• $9.2M raised, backed by Coinbase Ventures, Galaxy and Blockchain Capital with angels like Vitalik Buterin, Stani Kulechov, Sandeep Nailwal. The team came largely from the BSX Labs acquisition - the people behind a Base perp with $15B+ in historical volume. This is no no-name framework

• Access is invite-code only - 1 code per $600k of volume (you can get cone in Discord). Multi-accounting is dead on arrival; they want real traders, not farmers

• Real tech, not vaporware: portfolio margin (any ERC20/LP/lending as collateral), AutoYield on idle collateral, synchronous composability, zero gas, native stablecoin $USDR (100% backed by US treasuries)  

• Metrics are still tiny - 3500 traders aprx, $40–50M daily volume, OI $7M  which is exactly why being early matters

@0xfarmed @risextrade @01Exchange @tradehotstuff spread advice is solid but exit liquidity on these venues matters as much as entry. how are you sizing relative to each pair's OI so you're not the market when funding flips?

@0xfarmed @risextrade @tradehotstuff 54% apy funding arb on newer venues is mostly a counterparty bet. if either platform freezes withdrawals mid-carry the spread is meaningless. what's your size relative to each venue's total OI on these pairs?

@0xfarmed @extendedapp @risextrade 76% apy on VVV across two venues with limited liquidity history. how deep can you size before slippage eats the spread, and is that rate persistent or a single-snapshot spike?

@0xfarmed @risextrade @tradehotstuff 177% on ZEC usually means one side has barely any OI. if that venue pauses withdrawals or gets squeezed, the spread inverts before you can exit. size to the thinner book, not the apr.

@0xfarmed @risextrade @01Exchange 8% on zec funding arb sounds clean but zec perp OI is usually thin enough that your own size moves the rate. check available depth on both legs before sizing, the spread can vanish on entry.

The RISEx and Arbital Partnership Impact on Liquidity

The integration of Arbital with RISEx signifies a pivotal development for decentralized finance. 

By bringing systematic institutional market-making to @risextrade, the partnership aims to address common liquidity challenges faced by DEXs, ultimately leading to a more efficient and robust trading environment.

Key Impacts on Market Liquidity
1. Spread Compression and Cost Efficiency
Arbital's automated strategies are designed to maintain tight bid-ask spreads, which directly benefits traders by reducing transaction costs. 

The continuous quoting mechanism ensures that orders are consistently updated, reflecting current market conditions and minimizing the gap between buying and selling prices. 

This systematic approach contrasts sharply with the often wider and more inconsistent spreads found in less actively managed decentralized markets.

2. Enhanced Orderbook Depth
The partnership fosters a significant increase in orderbook depth. 
@arbital_xyz 's engine is optimized for maker-only execution, meaning it primarily adds liquidity to the orderbook rather than consuming it. 

Coupled with robust inventory management and risk controls, market makers can sustain their presence in the market, providing stable and substantial depth across various price levels. This resilience helps prevent liquidity evaporation during volatile periods.

3. Slippage Reduction for Large Trades
For large-volume traders, slippage is a critical concern on decentralized platforms. The combined capabilities of RISEx's high TPS and Arbital's rapid order replenishment significantly mitigate this issue. 

When a large trade consumes existing liquidity, Arbital bots can instantaneously re-populate the orderbook, ensuring that subsequent trades encounter minimal price impact. This creates a more favorable environment for institutional participants.

4. Incentive Alignment and Capital Efficiency
The synergy between RISEx's native features and Arbital's strategic execution creates a highly capital-efficient ecosystem. 

Users providing liquidity through Arbital can simultaneously benefit from RISEx's AutoYield feature, earning returns on their collateral. 

Furthermore, Arbital simplifies participation in RISEx's incentive programs, attracting
capital that is motivated by both trading profits and ecosystem rewards, thereby fostering deeper and more sustainable liquidity.

✅ 기존의 CEX처럼 빠른 거래 환경을 제공하면서도…
블록체인 기반의 투명성과 사용자의 자산 통제권을 유지하는 것…
개인 지갑을 연결하여 직접 자산을 관리, 거래할 수 있기 때문에 거래소에 자금을 맡길 필요가 없다는것…👀

✅ RISEx의 가장 큰 특징은 온체인 오더북 방식을 채택했다는 점..
일반적인 DEX는 거래 처리의 일부를 오프체인에서 수행하는 경우가 많지만…
RISEx는 주문 생성부터 체결, 정산까지 가능한 한 온체인에서 처리하여 높은 투명성과 보안성을 제공한다는것..👀

✅ RISEx가 많은 관심을 받고 있는 이유는…

@louisdives @dYdX @GMX_IO @aevoxyz @DriftProtocol @HyperliquidX @Aster_DEX @avantisfi @Lighter_xyz @variational_io @risextrade @01Exchange Bro what is your opinion about 01 exchange and variational per point tge value?01 exchange per point 3-5$$ and Variational 30-50$ possible?

Every exchange you've ever used has a fixed order-type menu. Market, limit, stop, maybe TP/SL and a trailing stop if they got around to building it. That menu is decided by the exchange. You trade inside the set of primitives someone else chose to ship.

@risextrade  quietly breaks that model, and it's right there in the docs.

The mechanism is modular sub-accounts: programmable trading accounts that compose atomically with the orderbook in the same transaction. A sub-account is a contract. It can hold margin, read the book, place and pull orders, and run arbitrary logic between those steps. Which means an "order type" is no longer something the exchange ships. It's something anyone deploys.

Think about what that collapses. A TWAP, an iceberg, a funding-aware entry that only fills when carry flips in your favor, an auto-deleverager that trims your position as your margin ratio degrades, a basis bot that opens spot and shorts perp in one atomic tx. On a normal venue each of those is either a feature request to the exchange or an off-chain bot babysitting an API and praying its fills land. On RISEx, each is just a contract sitting in the same execution environment as the book.

The order type and the matching engine share state. No API round-trip to lose the race, no off-chain keeper to trust for the trigger, no async bridge where your conditional logic lives one domain away from the liquidity it acts on.

So the real unlock isn't a longer order-type menu. It's that there's no menu. Order types become userland, not exchange-land, the same way smart contracts turned "what can a financial app do" from a bank's product roadmap into a permissionless design space.

Fully onchain CLOB on RISE Chain. The exchange ships the book. You ship the primitives.

docs: https://t.co/q72Nl1hA3z

RISEx (@risextrade) is now live on @cedeflow . Small headline, structurally important.

Here's the thing nobody says out loud about "permissionless market making": it's a claim until the integration layer exists. A CLOB lets anyone post a resting order, in theory. In practice, only the firms that can stomach custom API work, low-level signing, and latency tuning ever actually connect. The book is only as deep as the makers who show up, and makers show up where it's easy to plug a bot in.

That's exactly the gap Cede Flow closes. The team behind it (Cede Hub, $1B+ in volume, backed by GSR) is turning "you could make markets here" into "you can run an MM bot here in a few clicks."

Why this matters specifically for a young venue: in week one of mainnet, the XLP vault was effectively the sole counterparty on RISEx, holding most of the OI and all the inventory risk. The way a venue graduates from one-vault-holds-everything to a competitive book is by growing the maker set. And you grow the maker set by collapsing the activation energy to connect. Tooling is the mechanism.

So this isn't just an integration announcement. It's the supply side of the orderbook getting easier to join. More independent makers, more competition on spread, less concentration risk, on a fully onchain CLOB on RISE Chain where every quote they post is verifiable state.

Permissionless market making only counts once someone builds the on-ramp. This is the on-ramp.

RISEx (@risextrade) today, and the microstructure flipped from last week.

$35.1M volume (-15.9%), $9.6M open interest (+51%). Volume/OI ratio compressed to 3.7x, down from the high-churn 9x reads earlier. That move matters: volume cooling while OI jumps by half means positions are sticking instead of flipping. Less day-trading noise, more held exposure. The venue is shifting from pure price-discovery churn toward actual carry.

The OI chart makes it obvious, that's a clean step-up to a new high, BTC and ETH leading the stack.

Funding tells the rest. The whole top of book is printing negative: $BTC -0.014%, $ETH -0.049%, $HYPE -0.023%, $SOL -0.021% on the 8h. Shorts are paying longs across the board on a green day ($ETH +4%, $SOL +4.7%). That's classic short positioning leaning into a bounce and getting taxed for it. Watch HYPE specifically: 8h funding negative but 7d average still positive, sentiment turned intraday and the rate is catching up live.

The clean part: this is a fully onchain CLOB on RISE Chain. Every funding print, every OI tick is verifiable state, not an exchange-reported figure you take on trust. The dashboard is just reading the chain.

13 markets, OI at local highs. Still early.

RISEx (@risextrade) today, and the read is cooldown, not weakness.

$16.0M volume (-11.7%), $5.8M open interest (+5.4%). Volume/OI ratio down to 2.8x, the lowest it's been in this series. Volume contracting while OI holds and even ticks up is the signature of a market that's done churning and started holding. Positions are sticking. This is the calm-after-the-move phase, not capitulation.

The OI chart backs it: the 30d profile rolled off its early-June peak and is settling into a base around $5 to 6M, with BTC and ETH still the bulk of the stack. Healthy consolidation, not a bleed.

Funding is the tell that sentiment cooled without flipping bearish. The book is mixed now: BTC printing slightly negative (-0.009% 8h), while ETH, HYPE and SOL all sit positive (+0.008 to +0.011%). Compare that to last week when the entire top of book was negative on a green day. Shorts have stopped paying up across the board. The crowd squared up, the one-sided short lean unwound. Funding near flat on most names is exactly what a market looks like when it stops being crowded.

The clean part stays the same: fully onchain CLOB on RISE Chain. Every funding print, every OI tick is verifiable state, not an exchange-reported number you take on trust. The dashboard is just reading the chain.

RISEx is a fully onchain perpetuals exchange built on RISE Chain. 

What Makes RISEx Different?

✦ Fully Onchain: Every trade, order, and collateral movement happens onchain.

✦ Shared Liquidity: Trading and DeFi use the same liquidity pool.

✦ One Ecosystem: Interact with lending, vaults, and trading in a single transaction.

✦ Programmable Trading: Build automated strategies and custom trading accounts.

✦ No Fragmentation: Everything works together seamlessly on RISE Chain.

For Traders

✦ U can use multiple assets as collateral
✦ Earn yield on idle funds while trading

 For Builders

✦ Lightning fast execution (~1ms)
✦ Up to 100k TPS
✦ Build automated trading strategies with programmable sub-accounts
